title: Storage Type
description: A storage area (e.g. high-bay rack, block storage) that groups storage_points.

movement_policy.description: default_allow: manual areas, explicitly prohibit exceptions. explicit_only: conveyor areas, every route must be explicitly defined.

access_model.description: rack: rack location, individually addressable. block: block location, multiple load units, LIFO access. channel: channel/flow storage (e.g. satellite/drive-in racking), sequential access only - see channel_depth/entry_side/exit_side.

depth.description: For 'block': number of load units in depth.

stack_height.description: For 'block': number of load units stacked on top of each other.

access_order.description: direct: every storage_point individually reachable. lifo: Last In First Out (block location).

homogeneity_required.description: For 'block'/'channel': only one article may be in the block/channel at a time.

channel_depth.description: For 'channel': number of positions per channel, in depth. Positions can only be filled/retrieved in sequence.

entry_side.description: For 'channel': side from which load units enter the channel.

exit_side.description: For 'channel': side from which load units are retrieved. Same value as entry_side => LIFO channel (single-side access). Different value => FIFO channel (flow-through, e.g. gravity or satellite).

storage_point_generator.description: Generates storage_points from a grid instead of enumerating them individually.

layout_variants.description: Alternative, mutually exclusive bin configurations for the same physical bay footprint (e.g. a bay can be occupied by either 2 industrial pallets or 3 euro pallets side by side, but not both at once - the coordinate sets overlap physically). Use instead of storage_point_generator when a bay supports more than one load unit layout. Only one variant can be occupied per bay at runtime; enforcing that at booking time is a runtime/compiler concern, not part of this structure definition.

positions_per_bay.description: Number of load units of this type that fit side by side in one bay.

coordinate_pattern.description: e.g. '{aisle:02d}-{bay:02d}-EUR-{slot:1d}'. Must encode the variant so coordinates across variants don't collide as strings, even though they occupy the same physical space.

default_attributes.description: Attributes that apply to all generated storage_points, unless an exception overrides them.

allowed_load_unit_types.description: References ids in elements/load_unit_types.yaml. Restricts which load unit types may be stored at this storage_type.

capacity_volume.description: Usable volumetric capacity per storage_point, e.g. '1.5m3'. Not the same as size.width*depth*height: the usable cube is typically smaller than the outer envelope (rack beams, clearance, structural loss). 'size' describes the physical footprint/dimensions of the storage_point for fit checks; 'capacity_volume' is the nominal usable capacity for simple cube-sum checks (sum of load_unit_type.volume across stored goods <= capacity_volume), not a 3D bin-packing/geometric-fit calculation.

hazmat_classes.description: References ids in elements/hazmat_classes.yaml. If set, this storage_type is certified/restricted for these hazard classes; goods requiring a class not listed here must not be stored.

exceptions.description: Explicit deviations from the template for individual coordinates.

blocked_reason.description: References an id in elements/blocking_reasons.yaml. Required when blocked is true.

allowed_load_unit_types.description: References ids in elements/load_unit_types.yaml. Overrides the storage_type default for this coordinate.

sections.description: Subdivision of the storage type by properties (e.g. access frequency).

size.description: Outer physical dimensions (envelope) of the storage_point, used for geometric fit checks. Note: usable volume can be smaller than width*depth*height - see default_attributes.capacity_volume.

position.description: Position of the storage_point in the warehouse - either a logical reference (e.g. matching the coordinate_pattern) or physical coordinates in meters, measured from a fixed reference point of the building.
